methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Andrenam is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Matej Cernosek and Alex Chu that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies across public and private markets, including 2 publicly traded names (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms spanning Seed through Series A stages. Key private comparables include Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $10M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $25M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector and same/adjacent-stage companies—including Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Stark Defence ($500M), and Scale AI ($29.0B)—produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range of $60M to $29.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in Series A outcomes within the sector.

The two methods support a triangulated valuation range of $21M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $162M. The gap between stage-progression ($25M) and peer-median ($300M) signals material sensitivity to peer selection and stage normalization. Key uncertainties include traction relative to comparables, technology differentiation within the crowded defense-tech landscape, and regulatory pathway clarity.
